kpis.label: PUE
kpis.interpretation: Power Usage Effectiveness. Lower is better because less overhead energy is needed beyond the IT load.

kpis.label: REF
kpis.interpretation: Renewable Energy Factor. Higher is better because a larger share of demand is served from renewable energy.

kpis.label: ERF
kpis.interpretation: Energy Reuse Factor. Higher is better because more recoverable energy is reused instead of wasted.

kpis.label: CEF
kpis.interpretation: Carbon Emission Factor. Lower is better because each unit of energy carries less carbon intensity.

kpis.label: CO2 Savings
kpis.interpretation: CO2 Savings versus a baseline operating mode. Higher is better because more emissions are avoided.

kpis.label: PPW
kpis.interpretation: Performance per Watt. Higher is better because more useful work is delivered per unit of power.

kpis.label: CUE
kpis.interpretation: Carbon Usage Effectiveness. Lower is better because less carbon is associated with the IT energy delivered.

kpis.label: HUE
kpis.interpretation: Heat Usage Effectiveness. Higher values indicate better productive use of recovered thermal output.

kpis.label: SHE
kpis.interpretation: Sustainable Heat Exploitation. Higher values indicate stronger sustainable use of available heat streams.

kpis.label: APCren
kpis.interpretation: Adaptability Power Curve for Renewable Energy. Higher values indicate better renewable-aware operating flexibility.

kpis.label: DCA
kpis.interpretation: Data Centre Adaptability. Higher values indicate better operational adaptability under sustainability constraints.

outcome_statement: D3.2 sustainability KPIs connect operational telemetry, carbon intensity, renewable energy availability, and efficiency metrics into a single decision-support view for greener data-centre management.
